name.value: How do I add a site that is not already listed?

value.value: How do I add a site that is not already listed?

name.value: link at the top. There are two main options: Quick Add and Verified Add. The Quick Add lets anyone submit any site simply by entering the home page, although it needs to pass an approval process after submission before it is indexed. The Verified Add options can only be used by the site owner, who needs to verify that they own the site.

value.value: link at the top. There are two main options: Quick Add and Verified Add. The Quick Add lets anyone submit any site simply by entering the home page, although it needs to pass an approval process after submission before it is indexed. The Verified Add options can only be used by the site owner, who needs to verify that they own the site.

name.value: ) it isn't possible to notify you of changes to your submission directly. However, you can resubmit the site and see what the message is, i.e. it is now being indexed, still pending review, or rejected (along with rejection reason).

name.value: The indexing process respects the rules in robots.txt, so the indexing process begins by checking that file. Indexing then progresses accordingly, normally starting with the home page and following links to other pages within the same domain. The indexing process continues in a depth first manner until there are no further pages to index or until the indexing page limit for the domain is reached.

value.value: The indexing process respects the rules in robots.txt, so the indexing process begins by checking that file. Indexing then progresses accordingly, normally starting with the home page and following links to other pages within the same domain. The indexing process continues in a depth first manner until there are no further pages to index or until the indexing page limit for the domain is reached.

name.value: If you want to exclude certain pages from indexing, you would normally do this via robots.txt. Verified site owners can in addition configure their listing to exclude content based on:

value.value: If you want to exclude certain pages from indexing, you would normally do this via robots.txt. Verified site owners can in addition configure their listing to exclude content based on:

name.value: The following fields are used to determine how results are ranked: title, description, author, tags, url, content, indexed_inlink_domains_count, contains_adverts and owner_verified. There is further discussion of the relevancy tuning on some

value.value: The following fields are used to determine how results are ranked: title, description, author, tags, url, content, indexed_inlink_domains_count, contains_adverts and owner_verified. There is further discussion of the relevancy tuning on some

name.value: In summary, queries take the form /api/v1/search/<domain>?q=*, where parameters are:

value.value: In summary, queries take the form /api/v1/search/<domain>?q=*, where parameters are:

name.value: Results are returned in the following format, with all fields optional apart from id and url (which will have the same value):

value.value: Results are returned in the following format, with all fields optional apart from id and url (which will have the same value):

name.value: { "params": { "q": "*", "page": 1, "resultsperpage": 10, } "totalresults": 40, "results": [ { "id": "https://server/path", "url": "https://server/path", "title": "Page title", "author": "Author", "description": "Page description", "tags": ["tag1", "tag2"], "page_type": "Page type, e.g. article", "page_last_modified": "2020-07-17T00:00:00+00:00", "published_date": "2020-07-17T00:00:00+00:00", "language": "en", "indexed_inlinks": ["inlink1", "inlink2"], "indexed_outlinks": ["outlink1", "outlink2"], "fragment": ["text before the search ", "query", " and text after"] } ] }

value.value:
{
  "params": {
    "q": "*",
    "page": 1,
    "resultsperpage": 10,
  }
  "totalresults": 40,
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "https://server/path",
      "url": "https://server/path",
      "title": "Page title",
      "author": "Author",
      "description": "Page description",
      "tags": ["tag1", "tag2"],
      "page_type": "Page type, e.g. article",
      "page_last_modified": "2020-07-17T00:00:00+00:00",
      "published_date": "2020-07-17T00:00:00+00:00",
      "language": "en",
      "indexed_inlinks": ["inlink1", "inlink2"],
      "indexed_outlinks": ["outlink1", "outlink2"],
      "fragment": ["text before the search ", "query", " and text after"]
    }
  ]
}
